jokes.text: News! Bad! We're bankrupt. Oh, I borrowed money to invest in Bitcoin. Then the price crashed, and I sold at a huge loss. We're ruined! Oh, sorry. I didn't realize you were watching TV.
jokes.setup: Professor dramatically announces terrible financial news to the crew
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just apologizing for interrupting their TV watching, not about the bankruptcy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure - sets up one expectation (apologizing for bad news) then reveals the real situation (apologizing for interrupting TV). Classic Professor absurd priorities.

jokes.text: The tragic impact destroyed the planet Klarg, along with its 50 billion inhabitants. In other news, Morbo, guess what's back? The planet Klarg? Nope! The price of Bitcoin!
jokes.setup: News reports planet destruction with 50 billion deaths
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to celebrating Bitcoin price recovery with no concern for the genocide
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of media priorities and crypto obsession. The juxtaposition of mass death vs. Bitcoin price is perfectly callous in a Futurama way.

jokes.text: Haven't you learned that Bitcoin is just a pyramid scheme for rubes? Yes, and my plan is to exploit those rubes!
jokes.setup: Hermes tries to warn Professor about Bitcoin being a scam
jokes.punchline: Professor cheerfully admits he knows it's a scam and wants to exploit it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor logic - acknowledges the scam but sees it as an opportunity rather than a warning. Economical setup-punchline structure.

jokes.text: Uh, wha?!
jokes.setup: Bitcoin news treated as more important than planetary genocide
jokes.punchline: Morbo's confused reaction to the absurd news priorities
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that works well for Morbo's character but not particularly special

jokes.text: Yo. We're also leg brokers.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions owing money to loan brokers
jokes.punchline: Robot Mafia clarifies they also break legs (leg brokers)
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun on loan broker/leg breaker. Very economical delivery but relies on familiar wordplay.

jokes.text: It's a good thing we brought the oxen. Usually, we don't.
jokes.setup: Standard Western trope of bringing oxen for the journey
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they usually don't bring essential equipment
jokes.explanation: Understated absurdity about their typical lack of preparation. Good deadpan delivery but fairly simple concept.

jokes.text: You know, 'round these parts, folks don't think things. They reckon them. And instead of an idiot, I'll be known as a galoot!
jokes.setup: Fry tries to adopt Western dialect and customs
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically embraces being called a different word for idiot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - missing the point that galoot is still an insult. Shows his naivety and enthusiasm beautifully.

jokes.text: Well, anyway, I'm really looking forward to some quality father-son time. With who?
jokes.setup: Father expresses hope for bonding time with his son
jokes.punchline: Son's completely dismissive response shows their relationship
jokes.explanation: Economical setup showing dysfunctional father-son dynamic. The deadpan response is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: Um, is this thallium? Oh my, yes! High-grade ore! Um, Professor? Toxic. Relax. They're my prospecting teeth.
jokes.setup: Professor tastes potentially toxic ore to test it
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions he has special teeth just for tasting dangerous materials
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Professor moment - the casual way he mentions having specialized teeth for dangerous tasting is perfectly in character and original.

jokes.text: Welcome to Doge City! Looks like you got seven hornswogglers and a galoot!
jokes.setup: Western town greeting with period-appropriate insults
jokes.punchline: References earlier galoot joke and uses cryptocurrency name 'Doge'
jokes.explanation: Decent callback and crypto reference but fairly simple. The Doge/dog pun is obvious but works in context.

jokes.text: Your freest beverage. We're very poor. Gotcha. I'll just mix all the unfinished drinks together and start a tab.
jokes.setup: Fry asks for cheapest drink due to poverty
jokes.punchline: Bartender offers even worse solution - mixed leftover drinks on credit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their financial desperation. The bartender's solution is both disgusting and financially irresponsible.

jokes.text: At times, yes. Though her mood fluctuates like the price of Bitcoin.
jokes.setup: Fry comments that the bartender seems nice
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg compares her mood swings to Bitcoin's volatility
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy tying character behavior to the episode's cryptocurrency theme. Well-structured comparison joke.

jokes.text: Ill? You mean a hospital? A pet store? A brothel. A soup kitchen?! Yeah. A soup kitchen.
jokes.setup: Delilah euphemistically describes the brothel as a 'house of ill repute'
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent misinterpretations get increasingly absurd, ending with relief at 'soup kitchen'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry innocence - his complete inability to understand sexual references creates great escalating misunderstandings.

jokes.text: Out here in the West, I need something shootier. Like this gun-knife I invented-stole!
jokes.setup: Roberto explains why he has a gun instead of his usual knife
jokes.punchline: Admits to inventing then immediately stealing his own invention
jokes.explanation: Good Roberto character moment - his criminal logic of stealing from himself is perfectly insane. The compound word 'invented-stole' is economical.

jokes.text: Um, I'm not sure Leela has the skills to be a barmaid.
jokes.setup: Delilah offers Leela job as barmaid and/or prostitute
jokes.punchline: Fry only questions her bartending qualifications, not the prostitution
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - audience expects him to object to prostitution, but he only doubts her drink-serving abilities. Perfect Fry logic.

jokes.text: This burro is in near-mint condition. Perfect for hauling Bitcoin, light housework, or just abusing.
jokes.setup: Auctioneer describes donkey's practical uses
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions animal abuse as selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about animal cruelty fits Futurama's moral ambiguity, but the joke structure is fairly straightforward.

jokes.text: Fifteen! I always wanted to own a racehorse. That's a donkey, Bender. Donkeys are just ugly horses, Amy.
jokes.setup: Bender bids on what he thinks is a racehorse
jokes.punchline: Defends his mistake with completely wrong logic about donkey-horse relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Bender arrogance - refuses to admit mistake and doubles down with false confidence. His terrible logic is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It looks stupid. This whole trip is stupid. You're stupid, Pops!
jokes.setup: Father asks son's opinion about their camping spot
jokes.punchline: Son's response escalates from place to trip to personal attack
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of teenage angst. The repetition of 'stupid' builds nicely but the concept is fairly basic.

jokes.text: I wish Roberto were my dad. He's cooler than a green snake smoking a sugar cane vape. No self-respecting green snake would do that!
jokes.setup: Dwight uses ridiculous simile to praise Roberto's coolness
jokes.punchline: Father's literal response about snake behavior dignity
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist imagery with the vaping snake, followed by perfectly literal father response. The visual is memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Tooth test? Balderweeds.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions testing ore with his teeth
jokes.punchline: Assayer dismisses it with made-up Western expletive
jokes.explanation: Good made-up curse word that sounds authentically Western. Economical dismissal but not deeply funny.

jokes.text: But weed a million times that amount or we're gonna get clamped! Well, maybe there's some thallium in that there chassis of yours.
jokes.setup: Bender mentions Robot Mafia threat
jokes.punchline: Assayer suggests harvesting thallium from Bender's body
jokes.explanation: Good threat escalation and callback. The assayer's practical suggestion to mine Bender shows the casual dehumanization of robots.

jokes.text: Assay my shiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Assayer suggesting testing Bender's metal for thallium
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature catchphrase adapted to mining context
jokes.explanation: Perfect adaptation of Bender's catchphrase to the mining context. High character integration and economy.

jokes.text: 0.0000... Tain't even worth melting down. Well then, perhaps my ass might be of value to you in some other way?
jokes.setup: Assayer finds Bender's metal worthless
jokes.punchline: Bender desperately offers his ass for other purposes
jokes.explanation: Good Bender desperation moment. His willingness to prostitute his posterior is perfectly in character and mildly subversive.

jokes.text: Eh, it might make a good frying pan, but fried food makes me cackle.
jokes.setup: Considering uses for Bender's metal posterior
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific personal quirk about fried food causing cackling
jokes.explanation: Bizarre character detail. The specificity of the quirk makes it memorable and adds personality to minor character.

jokes.text: My motto is, 'He who smelts it, dealts it!'
jokes.setup: Assayer explains his all-in-one mining operation
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'he who smelt it, dealt it' fart joke applied to metallurgy
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay adapting childish joke to business context. Economical but relies on familiar reference.

jokes.text: Ooh, lookie there! Found another Bitcoin! Aw, dog dumplings. It's just a danged Ethereum.
jokes.setup: Excited discovery of cryptocurrency
jokes.punchline: Disappointment at finding 'lesser' cryptocurrency, treating digital currency as physical objects
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on cryptocurrency as mineable objects. The disappointment over Ethereum vs Bitcoin reflects real crypto culture.

jokes.text: What's with the jumbo prawn? A colleague. Mind if he sits in? Yes, I do mind! I value doctor-patient confidentiality!
jokes.setup: Roberto objects to Zoidberg observing his therapy session
jokes.punchline: Psychotic killer concerned about medical ethics and privacy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contradiction - the violent Roberto having normal concerns about medical privacy. Good irony in a killer respecting doctor-patient confidentiality.

jokes.text: I only got 30 cents in tips and one request to visit the back room. I mean, I wouldn't go, but it's nice to be asked. Can you think of anything I can do to look sluttier? Nope.
jokes.setup: Leela complains about poor performance as fake prostitute
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt 'nope' when asked how to look sluttier
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Leela's professional pride in fake sex work, capped by Fry's brutally honest assessment. Very economical punchline.

jokes.text: You know he's not actually a hero like in his books, right? He's a poker cheat and a low-down base mineral.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the Borax Kid's heroic reputation
jokes.punchline: Calling him a 'base mineral' instead of 'base person' - chemistry pun
jokes.explanation: Clever chemistry pun playing on 'base' meaning both immoral and chemical. Works well with Borax Kid's mineral-based identity.

jokes.text: Well, then how about a hand of Colorado Chump? As long as it's not poker.
jokes.setup: Fry refuses to play poker with known cheat
jokes.punchline: Agrees to obviously made-up game that's clearly also poker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry gullibility - falls for obvious deception because the game has different name. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Mumbles, go see what all that fuss is about. And wear my signature hat.
jokes.setup: Gunfight challenge outside
jokes.punchline: Sends sidekick to face danger while wearing his identifying clothing
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny cowardice - using sidekick as decoy/shield while maintaining plausible deniability. Shows Kid's true character perfectly.

jokes.text: Mumbles is dead! Don't argue with me, Mumbles. I'm the town doctor.
jokes.setup: Doctor declares Mumbles dead
jokes.punchline: Tells the corpse to stop arguing with his medical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - doctor's professional authority assertion to a corpse. Very economical and memorable.

jokes.text: Really? Do I get to die in your next book? Page three. Then you got yourself a galoot.
jokes.setup: Fry excited about being sidekick in adventure stories
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic about early death, uses callback to galoot reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry enthusiasm for his own fictional demise. Good callback usage and shows his simple priorities.

jokes.text: Can I sleep in the library? I'd like to say yes, but then it wouldn't be private.
jokes.setup: Son asks to sleep in father's library tent
jokes.punchline: Father's literal interpretation of 'private library' excludes even family
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation humor. Father's pedantic adherence to 'private' definition shows his social awkwardness.

jokes.text: This place has too much outdoors! It's all bugs and no Wi-Fi.
jokes.setup: Young person complaining about camping
jokes.punchline: Defining outdoors by lack of modern conveniences
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy. 'Too much outdoors' is economical and captures modern kid's perspective on nature.

jokes.text: Limbo stupid? Where did I go wrong? Probably when you took up limbo. From what I'm hearing, it's stupid.
jokes.setup: Father devastated that son thinks limbo is stupid
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact agreement based purely on overheard conversation
jokes.explanation: Good piling-on structure with Bender's casual cruelty. His willingness to judge without knowledge is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, you said shovels? I thought you said take a break and do nothing.
jokes.setup: Professor asks where the shovels Bender was supposed to pack are
jokes.punchline: Bender's obviously false mishearing excuse for laziness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender laziness excuse. The absurd mishearing is so obviously false it's funny, and completely in character.

jokes.text: Sometimes, I think a detachable ass is more a curse than a blessing.
jokes.setup: Bender needs to use his ass as a gold pan
jokes.punchline: Philosophical reflection on the downsides of his unique anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist philosophy about robot anatomy. The mock-profound tone about ass detachment is perfectly ridiculous and memorable.

jokes.text: Musical number: Bender and his ass sing cowboy song with extensive ass puns
jokes.setup: Bender and his detached ass travel together
jokes.punchline: Extended musical sequence built entirely around ass-related puns and wordplay
jokes.explanation: Ambitious comedic musical sequence. The commitment to the ass pun theme is impressive, and it's a memorable musical moment that stands out.

jokes.text: Oh, why didn't I listen to my own song?!
jokes.setup: Bender's song warned about the problems with ass riding
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequence proves his own song's wisdom right
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate callback to his own musical warning. Perfect ironic timing and very economical payoff.

jokes.text: This is a nice, quiet saloon with attached bordello.
jokes.setup: Complaint about noise disturbing the establishment
jokes.punchline: Describes brothel as casual business amenity
jokes.explanation: Good casual treatment of prostitution as normal business. The 'attached' description makes it sound like a garage.

jokes.text: Folks call me Amarillo Dwight. On account of I wrote a geography report on it. Done got me a 'A.'
jokes.setup: Dwight introduces himself with Western nickname
jokes.punchline: Nickname comes from elementary school geography assignment success
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - kid's pride in basic academic achievement as source of tough-guy nickname. Perfect blend of childish and Western.

jokes.text: Why not? They using Apple Maps?
jokes.setup: Roberto explains stagecoach won't reach destination
jokes.punchline: Kid assumes modern navigation technology failure
jokes.explanation: Simple contemporary reference joke. Economical but relies on familiar tech frustration rather than original humor.

jokes.text: But, won't that be, like, murder? 'Cause colleges look at stuff like that.
jokes.setup: Roberto's plan involves killing stagecoach drivers
jokes.punchline: Kid's concern is about college admissions, not morality
jokes.explanation: Excellent modern teenage priorities applied to old-west crime. The casual concern about college applications over murder is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Guess you ain't as cute as I thought.
jokes.setup: Dwight fails to stop stagecoach by lying in road
jokes.punchline: Roberto's matter-of-fact assessment of failed cuteness strategy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about failed child-endangerment plan. Roberto's casual disappointment in Dwight's lack of cuteness is perfectly psychotic.

jokes.text: Guess you ain't as cute as I thought.
jokes.setup: Plan relied on kid looking cute to stop stagecoach
jokes.punchline: Casual insult when plan fails
jokes.explanation: Good Roberto cruelty - blaming the kid for the plan's failure in the most insulting way

jokes.text: I think it's actually a cougar. Puma, cougar, whatever! Get him, Kid! Or a mountain lion. You know, they're really all the same animal.
jokes.setup: Dangerous cat threatens them, Fry calls it a puma
jokes.punchline: Leela's pedantic corrections during life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's need to be correct even in danger. The taxonomy lesson during attack is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: It seems I bravely shot that catamount just in time after Fry missed.
jokes.setup: Kid claims credit for saving everyone from the cat
jokes.punchline: Admission that he only shot after Fry had already missed
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of heroic moment. Kid's need to qualify his bravery reveals his actual cowardice perfectly.

jokes.text: There's barely any thallium! Just worthless gold.
jokes.setup: Disappointed by mining results
jokes.punchline: Calling gold worthless in context of their specific need
jokes.explanation: Good irony about gold being worthless when you need something specific. Fits the episode's theme

jokes.text: I mean his butt ass.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who performed the heroic surgery
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's clarification about which 'ass' he operated on
jokes.explanation: Simple clarification joke about anatomical vs. donkey ass. Fairly basic wordplay but fits the moment.

jokes.text: I steal it. I steal it not. I steal it...
jokes.setup: Bender contemplating whether to steal the valuable nugget
jokes.punchline: Uses 'loves me, loves me not' flower-picking format for theft decision
jokes.explanation: Creative adaptation of romantic trope for criminal decision-making. Perfect Bender approach to moral dilemmas.

jokes.text: And the daisy, too. I gotta know how it comes out!
jokes.setup: Roberto demands the thallium nugget
jokes.punchline: Also wants the flower to see the outcome of Bender's stealing decision
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Roberto's curiosity overriding his criminality. His genuine interest in the flower outcome is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Come on! He's picked up Bender's scent! His what now?
jokes.setup: Fry saying donkey can track Bender
jokes.punchline: Leela questioning how a robot has a scent
jokes.explanation: Good logical question from Leela. Simple but effective reality check on impossible situation.

jokes.text: This is the worst brothel I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: They discover the 'brothel' is actually a Bitcoin mining operation
jokes.punchline: Bender's disappointed review as if it were actually a brothel
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction treating the mining operation as failed sex work. Bender's expertise in both areas makes it work.

jokes.text: That's actually a pretty reasonable use case for robot heads.
jokes.setup: Discovery that robot heads are being used for Bitcoin mining
jokes.punchline: Amy's casual acceptance of robot exploitation as economically sound
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about robot rights and exploitation. Amy's business-minded acceptance of the ethical violation is casually disturbing.

jokes.text: I'm not your friend. Though, I do like you a lot.
jokes.setup: Bender calls Delilah their friend
jokes.punchline: She denies friendship while immediately contradicting herself
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing her emotional confusion. The immediate contradiction reveals her true feelings despite denial.

jokes.text: I'm not your friend. Though, I do like you a lot.
jokes.setup: Bender calling her friend
jokes.punchline: Denying friendship while admitting affection
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing her contradictory nature and emotional volatility

jokes.text: Mining Bitcoin with kidnapped robot heads? It's pure evil! Hardly. I donate all the proceeds to a local orphanage. Aw...
jokes.setup: Revelation of evil Bitcoin mining scheme using enslaved robots
jokes.punchline: Villain reveals charitable motivation, immediately softening everyone's reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection subverting villain reveal. The immediate sympathy switch shows how easily good intentions excuse bad methods.

jokes.text: But I can't let you tell anyone about my generous scheme, so I'll have to seal you in this comfortably air-conditioned warehouse. Forever! But I'll drop by every day with snacks. Aw...
jokes.setup: Delilah explains she must imprison them to protect her secret
jokes.punchline: Makes imprisonment sound like hospitality with amenities and daily visits
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the 'considerate villain' bit. Making life imprisonment sound like a spa treatment is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Any number you can think of, there's a number bigger than that!
jokes.setup: Bender describes the hell of cryptocurrency mining
jokes.punchline: His traumatized understanding of infinity through mathematical computation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant existential horror from crypto mining. Bender's traumatized discovery of mathematical infinity is both funny and philosophically interesting.

jokes.text: I deserved that.
jokes.setup: Bender gets hit after suggesting they burrow out with their hands
jokes.punchline: Immediate acceptance of deserved punishment for stupid suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical reaction. Bender's immediate acknowledgment of his stupid suggestion shows rare self-awareness.

jokes.text: What?! Well, now, there's nothing illegal about that. Those old stories are public domain. I change up a few words and claim the copyright for myself. Ya ever heard of Cinderella? That is so evil!
jokes.setup: Revelation that Borax Kid plagiarizes old stories
jokes.punchline: His defense citing public domain law, then casual mention of Cinderella plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Good satire of copyright abuse and literary plagiarism. The escalation to classic fairy tales makes it more egregious.

jokes.text: I'm too scared! There's bullets in every dimension!
jokes.setup: Father frozen in fear during gunfight
jokes.punchline: His scientific description of three-dimensional bullet distribution
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific fear response. His academic way of describing danger is perfectly nerdy and memorable.

jokes.text: Hang on! I'm limboing in!
jokes.setup: Dwight decides to help his father escape gunfight
jokes.punchline: Uses limbo dancing as rescue method in shootout
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup. Using limbo as heroic action is brilliantly absurd and shows character growth. Very original visual concept.

jokes.text: Can't we all just agree to use knives?
jokes.setup: Roberto complains about gun violence
jokes.punchline: Suggests knives as peaceful alternative, showing his weapon preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roberto character moment - his 'peace' proposal is just his personal weapon preference. Great ironic take on violence complaints.

jokes.text: I've never been so offended yet slightly flattered!
jokes.setup: Borax Kid offers to escort Leela to brothel to treat her wound
jokes.punchline: Leela's conflicted reaction to the inappropriate but gentlemanly offer
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing Leela's complex response to inappropriate chivalry. The contradiction captures realistic conflicted feelings.

jokes.text: I love you, Pops. And limbo is not entirely stupid.
jokes.setup: Father-son reconciliation after limbo saved the day
jokes.punchline: Dwight's grudging partial acceptance of limbo's value
jokes.explanation: Sweet character growth moment. Dwight's qualified praise shows his teenage reluctance to fully admit being wrong while acknowledging the truth.

jokes.text: If we ever get outta here, I'm gonna give this brothel a really bad Yelp review.
jokes.setup: Still trapped in the fake brothel/mining operation
jokes.punchline: Bender's modern solution to Old West imprisonment problem
jokes.explanation: Simple contemporary reference callback. The Yelp review threat is mildly funny but relies on familiar modern frustration rather than original humor.
